prompt: Diptych icon, deep black background split vertically. LEFT SIDE: a tiny 15mm cephalopod hatchling, clear glass body with COOL IRIDESCENT blue-green-silver shimmer across its skin like opal, two tall thin snail-like eye stalks, eight fine tentacles. RIGHT SIDE: the same creature 30 days later, slightly larger, now with WARM AMBER pigment spots beginning to bloom across its body over the fading iridescence — amber chromatophores emerging on a still-iridescent substrate. The transition from jewel to lantern visualized. Both creatures have the dominant tall eye stalks. Elegant macro detail.
